# react-unique-id-generator

> Unique IDs for React. Readable. Predictable. SSR-safe.

A lightweight, zero-dependency library for generating unique, sequential IDs in React applications. Built for accessibility (form labeling, ARIA), SSR hydration safety, automated testing, and performance monitoring. Works with React 16.8+.

## Why use this instead of React's `useId()`?

- Custom prefixes and suffixes (`"email-1"` instead of `":r0:"`)
- Provider-scoped counters for SSR isolation (no cross-request leaks)
- Works in React 16.8+ (not just React 18+)
- Works outside components (utility functions, server code)
- Automation test ID generation with naming strategies
- Cryptographically secure random IDs via `generateSecureId()`
- Scoped ID resets per feature/module
- Memory-efficient tracked IDs with auto-cleanup on unmount
- Performance monitoring with `useIdMetrics()`
- Overflow protection (warns at 1M IDs)
- Predictable, readable format

## Install

npm install react-unique-id-generator

## Quick Start

```tsx
import { IdProvider, useUniqueId } from 'react-unique-id-generator';

function App() {
  return (
    <IdProvider prefix="app-">
      <Form />
    </IdProvider>
  );
}

function Form() {
  const emailId = useUniqueId('email-');    // "email-1"
  const passwordId = useUniqueId('password-'); // "password-2"
  return (
    <form>
      <label htmlFor={emailId}>Email</label>
      <input id={emailId} type="email" />
      <label htmlFor={passwordId}>Password</label>
      <input id={passwordId} type="password" />
    </form>
  );
}
```

## Core API

- `<IdProvider prefix? suffix? startId?>` - Context provider that scopes ID counters to its subtree
- `useUniqueId(localPrefix?)` - Hook that returns a stable unique ID (scoped to nearest IdProvider)
- `useUniqueIds(count, localPrefix?)` - Hook that returns multiple stable unique IDs
- `useIdContext()` - Access the current provider's context
- `createServerIdManager(options?)` - Create an isolated ID manager for SSR (per-request)
- `generateAutomationId(componentName, options?)` - Generate deterministic test IDs
- `useAutomationId(componentName, options?)` - Hook version of generateAutomationId
- `AutomationIdPool` - Pre-allocated pool of automation IDs
- `nextId(prefix?)` - Legacy: generate next ID using global state

## v2.1 API

- `generateSecureId(length?)` - Cryptographically random ID (uses crypto.randomUUID/getRandomValues)
- `nextIdForScope(scope, prefix?)` - Sequential IDs within a named scope
- `resetIdForScope(scope)` - Reset counter for a specific scope
- `resetAllScopes()` - Reset all scope counters
- `getScopeCounter(scope)` - Get current counter for a scope
- `getActiveScopes()` - List all active scope names
- `useTrackedUniqueId(localPrefix?)` - Like useUniqueId but auto-cleans from registry on unmount
- `useTrackedUniqueIds(count, localPrefix?)` - Like useUniqueIds but auto-cleans on unmount
- `useIdMetrics()` - Hook returning real-time { totalGenerated, activeCount, peakActiveCount }
- `getIdMetrics()` - Non-hook version of useIdMetrics
- `getActiveIdCount()` - Number of currently tracked IDs
- `resetIdMetrics()` - Reset totalGenerated counter
- `cleanupInactiveIds()` - Clear the tracking registry
- `OVERFLOW_THRESHOLD` - Exported constant (1,000,000); dev warning fires when any counter exceeds this

## Documentation

- Full API reference: https://github.com/riyajath-ahamed/react-unique-id-generator#readme
- Detailed docs with all parameters: llms-full.txt
